title: read_notebook_file silently swallows non-string source cell items via filter_map
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
Perimeter Defense / Functional Correctness

## Location
src/tools/file/read.rs:205-271 (read_notebook_file source/output extraction)

## Violation
The function uses `filter_map(|v| v.as_str())` in four places (lines 208, 228, 244, 258) to collect source text or output text from the notebook JSON. If a cell's `source` array contains a non-string element (e.g. someone manually edited the .ipynb to include a number or object), that element is silently dropped — the displayed cell text is missing a line, but no error is raised. The model sees an incomplete cell and may edit based on the truncated content.

Also: line 232 falls into `=> continue,` for non-string non-array `text` field, returning the cell with no output shown but no warning. Read-then-edit cycles can produce diffs that surprise the model.

## Mandated refactor
Replace each filter_map with a fold that warns on non-string elements:
```rust
let (text, skipped): (Vec<&str>, usize) = arr.iter().fold((Vec::new(), 0), |(mut v, n), e| {
    match e.as_str() { Some(s) => { v.push(s); (v, n) } None => (v, n+1) }
});
if skipped > 0 { let _ = write!(output, "[Warning: {} non-string elements in cell {i} source]\n", skipped); }
```

Alternatively, define a SourceCell deserialization type that errors on malformed shapes at parse time instead of silently coercing.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
